title: ...and the Gunslinger Followed - We Deal in Lead (overview / review / game play) (solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k1SjNJaOJcA

Daniel (@dungeondive) finally gets a long-promised game to the channel: We Deal in Lead from Oddin Beard, a Cairn-based weird western he describes as very nearly an unofficial Dark Tower RPG — slip doors between worlds, an order of gunslingers descended from Arthur Eld, and a drifting reality that lets you graft in content from Runecairn or any other Cairn hack. The first stretch is a loving primer on the weird west itself: The Dark Tower (he's read the series seven times; Wolves of the Calla is his favourite King novel), Joe R. Lansdale's Dead in the West, Brautigan's The Hawkline Monster, the Merkabah Rider series, and the new splatter-western boom. Then the game: roll-under stats, deadly combat, and a shoot table where guns can miss, jam, or explode — sending you on a quest to reforge your weapon. The extended solo play is the treat. Preacher-gunslinger Brennan Groveshore tracks a thief called the Rogue Rose across a bleached desert, trades a favour with a healer named Ruth, sneaks past a slow mutant at a crumbling way station, and rolls into the town of Dusty Gulch — where mutilated cattle, a wrathful-moon oracle draw, and a stolen relic spin the hunt into something far more sympathetic: she may be a werewolf seeking a cure.

----

Daniel's oracle rolls turned a bounty hunt into a rescue mission mid-session. What's the best moment a solo oracle completely rewrote the story you thought you were telling?
